items.markdown:
An operational system refers to `the software infrastructure, databases, and frameworks that execute an organization's day-to-day commercial transactions` . Also known as Transaction Processing Systems (TPS) or Online Transaction Processing (OLTP) systems, they are built to maintain data integrity and support routine business functions efficiently.[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operational_system) [[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operational_system)[[2]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/computer-science/operational-system)[[3]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/operational-systems-and-informational-systems/)

Key Characteristics

- **Real-Time Processing:** Processes continuous data and transactions as they happen to provide up-to-the-minute updates.
- **High-Frequency Use:** Relied upon heavily by clerks, operational managers, and customers for short, atomic data operations.
- **Operational Efficiency:** Optimized for speed, reliability, and uninterrupted workflow execution.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/operational-systems-and-informational-systems/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/operational-systems-and-informational-systems/)[[2]](https://www.scribd.com/presentation/130348675/Operational-Information-System-Ppt)

Common Types & Examples Depending on the department, operational systems can be categorized into several core software infrastructures:

- **Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP):** Systems that manage core business operations such as manufacturing, inventory, procurement, and financials.
- **Customer Relationship Management (CRM):** Software used to track sales pipelines, customer interactions, and support tickets.
- **Order Management Systems (OMS):** Platforms that handle the lifecycle of an order, including split shipments and payment processing.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/operational-systems-and-informational-systems/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/operational-systems-and-informational-systems/)[[2]](https://marello.com/blog/erp-vs-dop/)[[3]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/information-systems-infrastructure-evolution-trends-david-mwendwa)[[4]](https://www.studeersnel.nl/nl/document/universiteit-van-amsterdam/business-operations-and-processes/operations-management-chapter-13-inventory-management/163142865)[[5]](https://www.arrow.net.au/glossary/)

Operational vs. Informational Systems Unlike informational systems (such as data warehouses) that are used by executives to analyze historical data and inform long-term strategy, operational systems exist purely to run the day-to-day business.[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operational_system) [[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operational_system)[[2]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/operational-systems-and-informational-systems/)| Feature[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operational_system)[[2]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/operational-systems-and-informational-systems/)[[3]](https://www.scribd.com/presentation/130348675/Operational-Information-System-Ppt) | Operational Systems | Informational Systems |
|---|---|---|
| **Primary Purpose** | Execution of daily transactions | Strategic planning and data analysis |
| **Typical Users** | Floor staff, clerks, frontline managers | Executives, business analysts |
| **Data Usage** | Detailed, current, continuously updated | Summarized, historical, queried periodically |

Management System Frameworks In a slightly different business context, an "operational system" (or Business Operating System) refers to the formal, documented frameworks companies use to align their strategies with daily execution cadence. Popular frameworks include **Lean Management**, **Six Sigma** , and **Scaling Up** (often implemented via software platforms to structure goal-setting and meeting rhythms).[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Business_operating_system_%28management%29) [[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Business_operating_system_%28management%29)[[2]](https://slack.com/blog/productivity/what-operations-management-really-does-and-why-it-matters)[[3]](https://www.acte.in/operations-management)[[4]](https://www.valuesdrivenachievement.com/blog/eos-metronomics-traction-which-operating-system-is-best-for-your-busines)
